serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Beyti Kebab (Tandoori Paneer) is approximately 200 g, comprising one flatbread wrap filled with tandoori-marinated paneer, vegetables, and tomato sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Beyti Kebab (Tandoori Paneer) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Beyti Kebab (Tandoori Paneer), cr_id G00010364, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from paneer composition using USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 173420, Cheese, cottage, creamed) as a supplementary reference for fatty acid breakdown, as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed kebab wrap with flatbread and paneer
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beyti Kebab (Tandoori Paneer). GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous mixed kebab wrap entries in the University of Sydney GI Database: the dominant carbohydrate source is flatbread (GI ~57 for white pita/lavash), substantially moderated by high fat (26.4 g/100g) and protein (10.7 g/100g) from paneer and marinade, which slow gastric emptying and blunt postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (45 × 10.48 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 4.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg/100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; sodium at 1080 mg/100 g (2160 mg per 200 g serving) represents 94% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit in a single serving, and consumption should only occur with explicit medical guidance and alongside a low-sodium dietary pattern for the remainder of the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g) and low sugar (2.85 g/100 g) are favorable, but high saturated fat (~14 g/100 g) from paneer may impair insulin sensitivity with regular consumption
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content and protein-rich paneer moderate postprandial glucose response (estimated GI 45), making occasional consumption acceptable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the high saturated fat content should be monitored as chronic high saturated fat intake is associated with worsened insulin resistance, and the flatbread wrap should ideally be substituted with a wholemeal alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4 g/100 g) with estimated saturated fat ~14 g/100 g, combined with sodium 1080 mg/100 g and cholesterol 85 mg/100 g, presents a significant cardiovascular risk profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this dish as an occasional food only; the combination of high saturated fat (~14 g/100 g), high sodium (1080 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (85 mg/100 g) can elevate LDL cholesterol and blood pressure simultaneously, and portion size should be strictly limited to no more than 100 g with no additional high-sodium or high-fat accompaniments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~14 g/100 g from dairy-based paneer exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold; sodium 1080 mg/100 g worsens inflammatory tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish; dairy-derived saturated fat at this level promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6), and the high sodium content exacerbates tissue oedema associated with active RA flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beyti Kebab (Tandoori Paneer) is a fusion dish combining the Turkish Beyti kebab tradition with South Asian tandoori cooking. The dish consists of tandoori-marinated paneer (Indian fresh cheese made from curdled whole milk) that is grilled in a tandoor oven, then wrapped tightly in a thin flatbread (lavash or similar), sliced into rounds, and served with a tomato-based sauce and sometimes yogurt. The paneer is marinated in a spiced yogurt mixture containing chili, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and garam masala before grilling, imparting a smoky, charred exterior with a soft, creamy interior. The dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at 302 kcal/100 g, with high fat (26.4 g/100 g, predominantly saturated from paneer at ~14 g/100 g), moderate protein (10.7 g/100 g), and low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g). Sodium is very high at 1080 mg/100 g, making it unsuitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The estimated glycemic index is 45 (low), moderated by the fat and protein content of paneer. Calcium content is notable at 118 mg/100 g from the paneer. This dish is commonly served as a lunch or dinner item in Middle Eastern and Indian fusion restaurants in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010364, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central paneer/cheese composition data as a supplementary reference. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, clearly documented.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (14.0 g/100 g) is estimated from paneer composition using USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 173420 and related paneer entries) as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this dish. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient vitamins, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific prepared dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; this is plausible given the minimal vegetable content and refined flatbread wrap. 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Beyti Kebab (Tandoori Paneer) was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. The dish is classified as Middle Eastern / South Asian fusion; multilingual names provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish requirements given SG region tag.
